page: Microsoft Azure B16as_v2 is a burstable virtual machine belonging to the Basv2 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an AMD EPYC 7763 processor with x86_64 architecture, providing 16 vCPUs mapped from 8 physical cores with 2 threads per core. The CPU operates at a clock speed of 3.525 GHz and includes 32 MB of L3 cache. The system is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory, yielding 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include any onboard GPUs or local storage, though it is Premium Storage capable for external disk attachments.

Benchmark results indicate strong single-core CPU performance, with a stress-ng single-core score of 2702.67, placing it in the top 25% of tested systems. Multi-core performance is average, as shown by a stress-ng multi-core score of 21607.66 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 10263.0. Memory performance is also strong, with a PassMark memory score of 2852.1, though large-block uncached read and write bandwidths fall into the average tier at 158.02 GB/sec and 79.4 GB/sec respectively. For language model tasks, the server achieves a strong text generation speed of 304.33 tokens/sec with a small 135M model, while medium 7B model inference remains average.

Because the B16as_v2 utilizes a burstable CPU allocation model, it offers a cost-effective profile for workloads with variable CPU utilization. The primary tradeoff is the potential for performance throttling once CPU credits are exhausted, making it less suitable for sustained, high-intensity compute tasks. The lack of local storage requires reliance on network-attached storage. This configuration is suitable for development environments, low-to-medium traffic web servers, code compilation, and database operations with intermittent load patterns.
description: Microsoft Azure B16as_v2 is an x86_64 burstable virtual machine from the Basv2 family, powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor running at 3.525 GHz. It features 16 vCPUs across 8 physical cores, 64.0 GB of RAM, and no local storage, though it supports Premium Storage. Operating under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this instance delivers strong single-core CPU performance and strong memory scores in benchmarks, while multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics remain average. Due to its burstable CPU allocation, the server provides qualitative cost efficiency for workloads with fluctuating resource demands. However, the risk of CPU throttling under sustained loads and the absence of local storage represent key tradeoffs. It is best suited for development environments, code compilation, and web serving with variable traffic.
og_description: Microsoft Azure B16as_v2 is a burstable virtual machine featuring 16 vCPUs on an AMD EPYC 7763 processor, 64 GB of RAM, and no local storage, offering strong single-core performance for variable workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure B16as_v2 is a burstable VM with 16 vCPUs, an AMD EPYC 7763 CPU, and 64 GB RAM, designed for workloads with variable resource demands.
tagline: A burstable x86_64 virtual machine featuring AMD EPYC processing and balanced memory for workloads with variable CPU utilization.
bullet_points: AMD EPYC 7763 processor with 16 vCPUs and 64.0 GB of RAM
Burstable CPU allocation model for cost-efficient variable workloads
Strong single-core CPU and memory benchmark performance
No local storage, requiring external Premium Storage attachment
Suitable for development, code compilation, and low-to-medium traffic web servers
